feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Seattle summers can cause ‘sunshine guilt.’ Here’s how to beat it
entry.summary: Seattleites are faced with a seemingly unique phenomenon: having too much to do during the summer months.

entry.title: Ebola death toll in DRC surges to at least 930 as outbreak gathers pace
entry.summary: Ebola spreading faster than any previous outbreak in DRC, with 37 new deaths recorded over just 24 hours at the weekend.

entry.title: Huge study finds first genetic clues for borderline personality disorder
entry.summary: Largest genetic analysis of the condition so far finds 11 linked locations in the human genome.

entry.title: Should You Keep Taking Vitamin D During the Summer?
entry.summary: While some people may get more time in the sunlight in the summer to improve their vitamin D levels, it is good to ensure you are getting enough vitamin D throughout the year.

entry.title: Mosquito season is here, in case your ankles haven't noticed. How humans are fighting back
entry.summary: Populations of two disease-carrying mosquito species are concerning public health officials, who are employing new strategies to reduce their populations.

entry.title: The First Thing a Dietitian Looks at on a Nutrition Label to See If a Food Is Healthy
entry.summary: Trying to find food that’s delicious and good for you can be a challenge, especially if you’re not sure what to look for on a nutrition label.

entry.title: Fruit Juice vs. Soda: Which Spikes Blood Sugar More?
entry.summary: Fruit juice and soda can both raise blood sugar quickly, but does one spike it more? Learn what the research says and how to make smarter choices.

entry.title: Sweeteners may slow down growth of gut bacteria
entry.summary: Researchers found that two very important gut bacteria were directly impacted by one particular sweetener.

entry.title: This GLP-1 retreat changed how I think about weight loss
entry.summary: There’s a lot to consider before starting or stopping a GLP-1 medication. This wellness retreat lays it all on the table.

entry.title: Strokes Are More Common During This Time Of Day — Here's Why
entry.summary: Plus, doctors share important stroke prevention advice.

entry.title: Boy, 13, one of Scotland's first to undergo innovative brain surgery
entry.summary: Toby Peart, 13, from Strathpeffer in the Highlands, was operated on by surgeons who were aided by "exceptional" 3D imaging technology.

entry.title: Alzheimer’s breakthrough: Scientists restore two hours of sleep without clearing brain plaques
entry.summary: Researchers have uncovered a surprising culprit behind sleep loss in Alzheimer’s disease: the brain’s own immune cells. In mice with amyloid plaques, overactive microglia triggered inflammation that kept the brain from getting enough deep, restorative sleep. …

entry.title: Counterpoint | Patients deserve the full story about antidepressants
entry.summary: They should be informed about potential benefits and risks, the available alternatives and what discontinuation may involve before treatment begins.

entry.title: Mom Includes Three Curious Golden Retrievers in Baby’s Fun Activity
entry.summary: Gathering on the living room rug for an afternoon book reading usually keeps little toddlers fully entertained. A viral clip by Pittsburgh creator @agoldendo...

entry.title: The surprising benefits of a five-minute dance break
entry.summary: From improved concentration to higher creativity, dancing offers a range of brain-boosting benefits. And you don't need to spend hours on the dance floor to feel the effects.

entry.title: New Review From American Heart Association Reveals The Optimal Daily Cups of Coffee For Your Health
entry.summary: A cup of coffee or two (or three) in the morning is one of the most common pleasures of the modern world.

entry.title: Five Cups of Coffee a Day Is Fine for Most Adults, Heart Association Says
entry.summary: Research has been mixed but association says evidence of coffee’s safety and even benefits has been growing over time

entry.title: My husband’s suicide shows there’s something very wrong with the U.S. insurance industry
entry.summary: “My love for Randy and my professional experience were not enough to save him,” a physician writes about her husband’s suicide.

entry.title: Medici ruler ‘killed with arsenic’ may have died from malaria
entry.summary: Francesco I de’ Medici’s brother was suspected to have killed him and his bride in 1587. DNA from bone samples suggest another explanation, experts say

entry.title: Scientists discover a protein that protects the brain from Alzheimer’s damage
entry.summary: A protein called SORLA may act as a powerful defense against the toxic tau tangles involved in Alzheimer’s disease. Mice with extra SORLA had less tau accumulation, less brain atrophy, and healthier connections between neurons, while mice without it experienc…

entry.title: Waking up with a headache every morning? Doctor says the timing may reveal the real cause
entry.summary: Health News: Most headaches come and go without causing serious harm. They often follow a stressful day, too little sleep, dehydration, or skipping meals. A glass .

entry.title: Scientists Create a 'Gut-on-a-Chip' That Reveals Hidden Drivers of Inflammatory Bowel Disease
entry.summary: Inflammatory bowel disease affects millions of people worldwide, yet scientists still don't fully understand what drives the chronic inflammation that damages the gut.

entry.title: Filtered coffee links to slower biological aging while instant coffee shows the opposite
entry.summary: Filtered coffee consumption was associated with slower biological aging, while instant coffee was linked to faster aging in UK Biobank participants. The cross-sectional findings do not prove causation, but they suggest that brewing method may influence coffee…

entry.title: The #1 Thing to Avoid If You Have High Blood Pressure, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Lifestyle modifications can be used to treat and prevent high blood pressure. Learn the number one thing to avoid if you have high blood pressure.

entry.title: Here's Exactly How Much Sleep To Get Each Night if You're Over 60, According to Experts
entry.summary: Plus, why you may find yourself naturally waking up earlier.

entry.title: Modern Life May Be Pushing Human Minds Beyond Their Limits, Reflecting an Evolutionary Mismatch
entry.summary: Try to think back to the last day you remember that wasn't packed full of things to do, people to see, and places to go.

entry.title: Higher consumption of sweeteners linked to decline in memory
entry.summary: Researchers discovered adults who consumed heavier amounts of sweeteners showed a 62% faster decline.

entry.title: Doctor warns these 7 everyday habits can shorten your health span after 50, most people ignore them
entry.summary: Turning 50 often changes the way people look at life. Children may have grown up, careers may be more stable, and many finally find time for themselves. Yet, while life may seem to slow down, the body quietly enters one of its most important phases.This is th…

entry.title: Malaria Vaccine's Achilles' Heel: Its 4th Dose
entry.summary: Getting parents in Africa to bring their kids back in for that final booster shot has been a challenge

entry.title: How Flossing Impacts More Than Just Your Oral Health—What to Know
entry.summary: Dental experts share the effects of flossing on your overall health—and why you should be practicing this habit daily.

entry.title: NC farmers see increased produce sales as cyclosporiasis outbreak continues
entry.summary: Shoppers are turning to locally grown produce sold at the State Farmer’s Market as health officials investigate the cyclosporiasis outbreak in North Carolina to find a source.

entry.title: An avocado a day keeps the doctor away: new study
entry.summary: New research has found the brunch menu staple is associated with a major health benefit for the heart.

entry.title: Tracking Cyclosporiasis: 30 more in Wake diagnosed with diarrhea-causing bug
entry.summary: More than 30 people got a Cyclosporiasis diagnosis on Thursday in Wake County, pushing the total number of people dealing with the diarrhea-causing bug to 270.

entry.title: You don't have to drink alcohol to damage your liver: Doctor reveals 9 unexpected factors that could be putting it at risk
entry.summary: Most people think about the liver only when alcohol enters the conversation. But the truth is far more surprising. The liver is one of the hardest-working organs in the body. Every day, it filters blood, breaks down medicines, stores nutrients, regulates chol…

entry.title: Sweeteners may slow down growth of gut bacteria
entry.summary: Researchers found that two very important gut bacteria were directly impacted by one particular sweetener.

entry.title: Heavy TV watching associated with smaller brain structures, study finds
entry.summary: "Turn off that TV, it'll rot your brain!" has been a household refrain for decades. While "rot" might be too strong a term, researchers are finding that the overall sentiment could have some merit.

entry.title: Stomach bugs surging as doctors say certain travel essentials could save your trip
entry.summary: With travelers' diarrhea the most common illness during summer travel, the CDC says packing oral rehydration salts and loperamide can help manage symptoms.

entry.title: Second person dies of Legionnaires’ disease as New York City cases decline sharply
entry.summary: A second person has died of Legionnaires’ disease in connection with an outbreak on New York City’s Upper East Side that has sickened 72 people and prompted cleanup efforts at the world-famous Metropolitan Museum of Art and Guggenheim Museum.

entry.title: The Best After-Lunch Drink for Better Digestion
entry.summary: If you’re looking to support your digestion after lunch, a warm cup of ginger tea may be the best option. Here’s what dietitians have to say.

entry.title: Tal Bagels, Popular UWS Bagel Spot, Closed by the Health Department
entry.summary: The Health Department closure sign on the front of Tal Bagels on the Upper West Side. Photo by Nate Jones.

entry.title: Is intelligence related to happiness?
entry.summary: We often imagine the brooding genius burdened by intellect, or assume that ignorance is bliss. But what does the data say? Decades of psychological research reveal a highly complex relationship between cognitive ability, emotional skills, and human joy.

entry.title: Can Intermittent Fasting Help Reduce Visceral Fat?
entry.summary: Some dietary changes and patterns, like intermittent fasting, may be effective for shrinking visceral fat, which could protect your health.

entry.title: My Husband Magically Developed a Rogue STI. He Swears He Didn’t Cheat, but, Uh, What the Hell?
entry.summary: I want to believe his doctor, but I'm not convinced.

entry.title: The ‘ice cream paradox’ — how the treat can actually be good for your health
entry.summary: With Sunday marking National Ice Cream Day, experts are giving the scoop on how the sweet summer treat can actually be good for your health.

entry.title: 24 GLP-1 Users Reveal The Good, The Bad, And The Shocking Side Effects
entry.summary: "I’m the one that has to live in my body, I’m doing what I need to get my life back, and that’s all that matters."

entry.title: Potato Salad vs. Pasta Salad: Which Is Better for Blood Sugar?
entry.summary: If you’re watching your blood sugar, dietitians say you don’t have to choose between potato salad and pasta salad. Here’s why.

entry.title: This Medication Is Everywhere—And It May Slow Biological Aging
entry.summary: New research suggests that GLP-1 medications may slow the aging process. Here's why.

entry.title: Robot doesn't perform the surgery: Cardiac surgeon debunks the biggest myths around robotic heart surgery
entry.summary: Health News: \"Knowledge is power,\" said philosopher Francis Bacon, and nowhere is that more relevant than when it comes to heart surgery. Hearing the words robotic.

entry.title: Simple Ways to Prevent Age-Related Muscle Loss
entry.summary: A video outlines practical ways to slow age-related muscle decline and why accessible routines matter for daily function. It details how strength training tw...

entry.title: I Have a Genetic Risk for Alzheimer’s. I Made These 5 Lifestyle Changes to Preserve My Brain Health
entry.summary: After her father’s Alzheimer’s diagnosis, one woman learned she had a rare genetic risk for the disease. She made major lifestyle changes to maintain brain health.
